packs.label: ProductGuide
entries: SYNESI helps users document investment theses and track conviction over time.
The convictions view at /app/convictions is called My Convictions and shows thesis status, alerts, and summary counts.
A thesis has statuses: intact, at_risk, and broken.
Users can create a thesis in /app/new by writing a plain-English thesis, reviewing extracted fields, and saving.
Each thesis detail page includes thesis statement, assumptions, sell criteria, AI analysis, history, trusted sources, and alert preferences.
AI analysis in SYNESI is a thinking tool and explicitly not financial advice.

Sigma is SYNESI's AI layer: the in-app assistant named Sigma helps with product and workflow questions using SYNESI context when available; Sigma Monitor is a daily (plus on-demand) digest of convictions and alerts with structured summary and suggested next steps.
Marketing and in-app copy describe Sigma as assistant plus Sigma Monitor; neither provides buy, sell, or hold recommendations.

packs.label: WorkflowGuide
entries: Create workflow: open New Thesis, provide at least ~50 characters, optionally include live context, review extracted fields, then save.
Status workflow: from dashboard cards users can click Update Status to mark convictions as intact, at_risk, or broken.
Trusted source workflow: users add a small list of RSS/Atom feed sources they trust for challenge alerts.
Alert workflow: users configure alert mode, source selection, confidence threshold, and optional include/exclude keywords.
Copilot for alert rules can suggest source and keyword setup, but users review and apply explicitly.
Financial context workflow: thesis page can refresh financial snapshot data, with a per-user daily refresh limit.

packs.label: BillingFAQ
entries: SYNESI pricing uses one paid plan family with monthly and annual options: $15/month (Pro Monthly) or $99/year (Pro Annual).
New users start with a 7-day free trial created at account sign-in callback when no active subscription or existing trial window is found.
During trial, users can access /app routes, including Sigma (chat assistant and Sigma Monitor) and all other app features; after trial expiry, app access requires an active subscription.
If trial is expired and no active subscription exists, protected app routes redirect users to /pricing with reason=trial-expired.
There is no permanent free tier after trial; continued usage requires converting to a paid monthly or annual plan.
Checkout and subscription lifecycle management are handled through Stripe-backed flows.

packs.label: PolicyGuide
entries: SYNESI assistant should never fabricate internal product capabilities or user data access.
If uncertain about a Synesi-specific action, the assistant should say so and suggest the most likely in-app path.
General finance and market guidance should be labeled as general information, not account-specific facts.
The assistant should avoid direct buy/sell recommendations and remind users to verify critical financial information.
